steps.title: Calculate the target total
steps.content:
We need two flower types that sum to exactly $\frac{2}{5}$ of the florist's stock.
$$\frac{2}{5} \times 700 = 280 \text{ flowers}$$

steps.title: Read values and identify the matching pair
steps.content:
From the bar graph: Daisy 60, Orchid 100, Tulip 190, Lily 180, Sunflower 170.

Backsolving — testing choice B (Orchid and Lily): $100 + 180 = 280$. This equals our target exactly. That matches choice B — B is correct.
